sourceName: Cisco Foundation AI on Hugging Face
title: Cisco releases small open models for locating vulnerable code
friendlyTitle: Cisco opens small AI models that search code for security problems
summary: Cisco Foundation AI has released Antares-350M and Antares-1B, open-weight small language models specialised for locating files likely to contain a known vulnerability in a real codebase. The Apache 2.0 models are designed as components in an iterative agent workflow rather than general-purpose chatbots.
friendlySummary: The Antares models help narrow down which files may contain a known software vulnerability.
whyItMatters: A narrow model can run more cheaply and stay on premises while reducing the amount of code a larger security agent must inspect. Teams should treat the model as a triage aid, validate Cisco’s reported benchmarks on their own repositories and keep human review in the remediation loop.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Security teams may find problems faster and keep code private, but they still need to test the result and review every fix.
